title: TMS-EEG-MRI-fMRI-DWI data on paired associative stimulation and connectivity (Shirley Ryan AbilityLab, Chicago, IL)

funding_references.funder_name: Dr. Ralph and Marian Falk Medical Research Trust

description: This dataset comprises multimodal neuroimaging data from a bifocal transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) study investigating cortico-cortical paired associative stimulation (ccPAS) and brain connectivity. Participants underwent TMS-EEG recordings targeting bilateral primary motor cortex with varying interstimulus intervals (4, 9, or 14 ms), combined with structural and functional MRI acquisitions. The study examines spike-timing-dependent plasticity mechanisms for modulating interregional effective connectivity in the human brain.
methods_description: Data collection involved MRI-navigated bifocal TMS-EEG sessions with 64-channel EEG recorded in DC mode (right mastoid reference). ccPAS was delivered with left M1 stimulation at t=0 ms followed by right M1 stimulation at asynchronies of 4, 9, or 14 ms across three sessions. Single-pulse TMS (spTMS) was applied at 100% or 110% resting motor threshold before and after ccPAS (10 and 60 minutes post-stimulation). Resting-state EEG and structural MRI (T1w) were acquired from all subjects; some subjects additionally received resting-state fMRI and high-angular-resolution diffusion imaging (HARDI, 271 directions).
